title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: Mass shooting leaves 12 dead and at least 9 hurt in an impoverished South Africa community
stories.summary:
A violent mass shooting in an impoverished neighborhood near Johannesburg has left 12 people dead and at least nine others injured. Police confirmed that an armed gang opened fire on the community late Tuesday before escaping the scene in a minibus.

Authorities have launched a manhunt for at least ten suspects believed to be responsible for the attack. The incident occurred within an informal settlement in Cleveland, highlighting ongoing security challenges in South Africa's largest city.

links.headline: Mass shooting with at least 10 attackers in Johannesburg
links.excerpt: A manhunt is underway for at least 10 suspects in a mass shooting that left 12 people dead near Johannesburg.
links.imageAlt: Mass shooting with at least 10 attackers in Johannesburg

links.reliabilityBasis: source-prior=medium; article-text: no reliability modifier cues

links.headline: Manhunt under way in South Africa after 12 killed in mass shooting in Johannesburg
links.excerpt: Another nine people were injured at an informal settlement in Cleveland late on Tuesday.

links.excerpt: An armed gang opened fire in a poor neighborhood in South Africa's biggest city, killing 12 people and wounding at least nine, before fleeing in a minibus, police said Wednesday.

stories.title: Trump Warns Iran the Time for Talks Is Over: Act Now or 'Pay the Price'
stories.summary:
Trump Warns Iran the Time for Talks Is Over: Act Now or 'Pay the Price'. Iran will “pay the price” for taking “too long” to negotiate a Middle East peace deal, President Donald Trump cautioned Wednesday, making his pledge in the wake of Washington and Tehran exchanging fire following the downing of an American helicopter over the contested waters of the Strait of Hormuz.

The post Trump Warns Iran the Time for Talks. BBC: Trump says US will hit Iran 'hard' again today.

BBC reports that the US president warns Iran "will have to pay the price" for taking too long to agree a deal, after Tehran vows retaliation to any attacks.

links.excerpt: The US president warns Iran "will have to pay the price" for taking too long to agree a deal, after Tehran vows retaliation to any attacks.

stories.title: Top secrets come spilling out
stories.summary:
Top secrets come spilling out.

In 1917, British analysts deciphered a coded message the German foreign minister sent to one of his country's diplomats vowing to begin "unrestricted submarine warfare" and seeking to win over Mexico with a promise to "reconquer the lost territory in Texas, New Mexico and Arizona" if the US entered the world war.

When it became public, the Zimmerman Telegram caused a sensation, helping propel the US into the conflict against Germany.

Not one Democrat voted yes. Republicans wasted zero time going for the jugular.

stories.summary:

Australian activists detained by Israel while aboard a flotilla attempting to deliver aid to Gaza return to Sydney, with organisers alleging abuse, sexual assault and beatings, some of which reportedly left detainees hospitalised.

stories.summary:
Manila Water is highlighting the critical role of climate-resilient infrastructure as the Philippines faces a combination of southwest monsoon conditions and El Niño. The company points to the Upper Wawa Dam as a primary example of such essential investment.

This facility serves as a vital component in ensuring long-term water security for the region. It is specifically designed to provide reliable water supply while simultaneously enhancing flood control measures during shifting weather patterns.

stories.title: PH Army denies internal discontent, says troops remain professional
stories.summary:
PH Army denies internal discontent, says troops remain professional. MANILA, Philippines — The Philippine Army on Wednesday rejected reports of alleged internal discontent within its ranks, saying it remains a disciplined and professional force.

Philippine Army Commanding General Lt.

stories.title: DepEd seeks P245.8M for school repairs, cleanup in quake-hit Mindanao
stories.summary:
DepEd seeks P245.8M for school repairs, cleanup in quake-hit Mindanao. MANILA, Philippines – The Department of Education has estimated an initial requirement of P245.8 million to cover cleanup operations, debris removal, and repair work in schools affected by the recent Mindanao earthquake.

In Situation Report No. 7 on Wednesday, an estimated P215.698 million is needed to repair damaged classrooms, alongside P30.121 million allocated for cleanup.

stories.title: Aid extended to victims of Mindanao quake
stories.summary:
Relief efforts are underway in Mindanao following a magnitude 7.8 earthquake that devastated local communities. The Razon Group has mobilized significant resources to support affected residents.

Companies under the group, including Primelectric, ICTSI, and Manila Water, have led the distribution of essential aid. This assistance includes generator sets, cash relief, and other critical supplies necessary for recovery.

stories.title: The Guardian view on NHS records: patients are not raw material for big tech | Editorial
stories.summary:
The Guardian view on NHS records: patients are not raw material for big tech | Editorial.

Ministers should end Palantir’s contract before medical confidentiality is sacrificed to Silicon Valley’s appetite for public data Alarm bells ought to have rung when it emerged last month that Palantir engineers could gain “ unlimited access ” to identifiable NHS patient data.

Such sensitive medical information was only supposed to be available eithe.

stories.title: Experts say we should use passkeys, but can a smartphone PIN really be safer than a password?
stories.summary:
The Guardian's long-running reader Q&A series recently tackled a pressing question regarding personal cybersecurity and the adoption of passkeys. A contributor expressed confusion over how authentication methods like smartphone PINs or facial recognition could be considered safer than traditional passwords.

This inquiry highlights the ongoing debate surrounding modern digital security protocols. While experts advocate for the widespread use of passkeys to reduce vulnerability to phishing and data breaches, many users remain skeptical about their practical implementation.

links.headline: Experts say we should use passkeys, but can a smartphone PIN really be safer than a password?
